meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Skills Setup to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. Master Claude Code and Antigravity with this expert-led, step-by-step beginner checklist designed for student developers.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' barrier, master Claude Code for rapid iteration and Antigravity for project scaffolding. By automating boilerplate and focusing on complex logic, you shift from 'coding student' to 'AI-augmented engineer,' producing high-quality, deployable projects that demonstrate real-world technical competency to future employers.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a graveyard for potential. You have the theoretical knowledge, but every job description demands a portfolio you haven't built yet. The gap between knowing syntax and shipping a production-ready application feels like a chasm, leaving you stuck in 'tutorial hell' while the industry moves at breakneck speed.

Enter the era of the AI-augmented developer. By integrating Claude Code into your workflow, you aren't just writing code—you are orchestrating architecture. This checklist transforms your development process, allowing you to bypass the trivial setup phases and focus on building high-impact features that actually get you hired.

steps.title: Environment Orchestration
steps.description: Establish a clean, professional development environment using Antigravity to define your stack, ensuring your project structure follows industry-standard patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional SaaS project structure with Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript. Include a modular directory structure that separates concerns for components, hooks, and API routes.

steps.title: Database Schema Definition
steps.description: Define your data models to support a real-world application, ensuring your backend is robust enough to handle authentic user interactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a task management application including User, Project, and Task models with appropriate relational constraints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npx prisma init && npx prisma db push'

steps.title: Feature Logic Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on core business logic, transforming abstract requirements into functional, tested code blocks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure server-side action to handle user authentication and profile creation, including input validation using Zod.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Implement secure auth action with Zod validation'

steps.title: UI Component Assembly
steps.description: Translate your design vision into a responsive, accessible interface by leveraging AI-assisted component generation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a responsive dashboard sidebar and navigation component using Shadcn UI patterns and Lucide icons.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Generate Sidebar component with responsive state management'

steps.title: Automated Testing & QA
steps.description: Solidify your portfolio by including automated tests, a hallmark of senior-level development that distinguishes your work from amateur projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the authentication service using Vitest, covering success and error scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Write vitest unit tests for the auth service'

pro_tips: Always provide Claude Code with your existing 'README.md' to ensure context-aware code generation that matches your current project architecture.
Use Antigravity to enforce strict linting rules; employers judge code by its consistency, not just its functionality.
Commit your code after every successful Claude Code interaction to create a narrative of progress in your GitHub contribution graph.
Refactor AI-generated code manually at least once; understanding the 'why' behind the code is what allows you to pass technical interviews.
Treat your project documentation as a marketing asset; use Claude to write professional 'How to contribute' sections.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Railway, write a 'Lessons Learned' blog post on Dev.to, and link the live demo in your LinkedIn profile.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' time to start. You have the tools, the guide, and the capability to build a portfolio that lands you your first engineering role. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
